title: Tilray Sinks 4%: 3 Reasons the Market Isn't Impressed Despite Record Q3 Revenue
text: Tilray Brands stock declined 4% following Q3 earnings despite posting record revenue, reflecting investor skepticism about the company's path to profitability. Q3 net revenue reached $206.7 million, exceeding consensus expectations of $201.3 million. This represented a beat, but failed to drive positive market sentiment. The critical disappointment came in profitability metrics. Adjusted EPS showed a loss of $0.24 versus the anticipated $0.14 loss—a significant miss of approximately 71%. Revenue growth has not translated into earnings improvement. The substantial EPS miss underscores that revenue growth is not yet translating into sustainable earnings. TD Cowen responded by cutting its price target from $10 to $7. TLRY stock has lost 97% over five years through repeated equity issuances and acquisitions. Recent challenges include the BrewDog acquisition (purchased for approximately $53 million, previously valued at $2 billion) and a $13 million lawsuit from the Bob Marley estate regarding unpaid licensing fees. U.S. cannabis rescheduling—a major bull case driver—continues moving slowly. Meanwhile, the company's beverage pivot remains unproven at commercial scale, despite partnerships with Carlsberg and recent product launches. Management noted dramatic improvements: Q3 net loss of $25.2 million versus $793.5 million year-over-year, plus reaffirmed FY2026 adjusted EBITDA guidance of $62-72 million. International cannabis revenue surged 73% annually.
